about.title: About Nearshore Navigator
about.subtitle: Your trusted partner for industrial expansion in Mexico
about.mission: We help US companies establish successful manufacturing operations in Baja California, Mexico. With decades of combined experience in industrial real estate and cross-border logistics, we are your single point of contact for nearshoring success.
about.expertise: Our team brings together expertise in industrial real estate, Mexican labor law, customs and trade compliance, and manufacturing operations. We've helped dozens of companies — from Fortune 500 enterprises to innovative startups — navigate the complexities of manufacturing in Mexico.
about.team: Based in both San Diego and Baja California, our bilingual team understands the nuances of doing business on both sides of the border. We're not just consultants — we're your partners in growth.
about.founderQuote: My experience includes leadership work in market-entry environments—where execution, timelines, and operational details matter.
about.founderTitle: Expert-led nearshoring guidance for Baja California
about.founderDescription: We help manufacturing companies expand into Baja California with a clear process, local market insight, and a vetted network behind you. Depending on your needs, support can include industrial site selection, contract manufacturing partner matching, or a faster shelter route—so you move forward with confidence and fewer surprises.
about.founderStat2: Supported the setup of 20+ companies in Mexico across multiple industries
about.founderStat3: Hands-on support from evaluation to visits, due diligence, and launch momentum
about.heroSubtitle: Built for manufacturers entering Baja California. We provide structured guidance, practical options, and the right introductions so your expansion doesn’t stall in uncertainty.
about.shelterServicesDesc: For when speed matters. We help you assess the most efficient entry route (Shelter, M&A, or standalone) and connect you with vetted options.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.section1_text: We collect information you provide directly to us, such as when you fill out a contact form, request a consultation, or subscribe to our newsletter. This may include your name, email address, phone number, company name, and any other information you choose to provide.
privacy.section2_text: We use the information we collect to provide, maintain, and improve our services, to communicate with you about our services, and to send you marketing communications (with your consent).
privacy.section3_text: We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer your personal information to third parties without your consent, except as necessary to provide our services or as required by law.
privacy.section4_text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction.
privacy.section5_text: We use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your experience on our website, analyze site usage, and assist in our marketing efforts.
privacy.section6_text: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at

We act as intermediaries connecting clients with service providers and do not assume liability for third-party services.

distributionPage.heroSubtitle: Optimize your supply chain with cross-border fulfillment and 3PL solutions at the busiest land port of entry in the Western Hemisphere.
distributionPage.sameDayDesc: Reach 40 million consumers in Southern California within hours.
distributionPage.section321Desc: Ship duty-free to US consumers on orders under $800.
distributionPage.portsDesc: Otay Mesa and San Ysidro crossings for flexible routing.
distributionPage.transloadingDesc: Switch containers between rail, truck, and air seamlessly.
distributionPage.strategicText1: Baja California is adjacent to San Diego, providing immediate access to the US Interstate highway system (I-5 and I-805). The Otay Mesa Commercial Port of Entry processes over $50 billion in trade annually, making it the busiest commercial crossing on the California-Mexico border.
distributionPage.strategicText2: For e-commerce brands targeting the US West Coast market, Baja California acts as the perfect fulfillment hub — combining Mexican labor cost advantages with same-day delivery capabilities to Southern California.
distributionPage.faq1Question: What is Section 321 and how does it benefit e-commerce?
distributionPage.faq1Answer: Section 321 is a US customs provision that allows duty-free import of goods valued under $800 per recipient per day. By shipping from a Baja California fulfillment center directly to US consumers, e-commerce brands can avoid import duties entirely on qualifying orders.
distributionPage.faq2Question: How long does it take to cross the border?
distributionPage.faq2Answer: FAST-lane enabled carriers with C-TPAT certification can cross in under 30 minutes during off-peak hours. Standard commercial crossings average 1-2 hours. We work with 3PL partners that have priority crossing privileges.
distributionPage.faq3Question: Can you handle cold chain / temperature-controlled goods?
distributionPage.faq3Answer: Yes. Several of our 3PL partners offer temperature-controlled warehousing and transportation for food, pharmaceuticals, and other perishable goods requiring cold chain integrity.
distributionPage.guideDesc: Save up to 25% on tariffs by fulfilling US orders from Tijuana.
distributionPage.questionnaireDesc: Complete our 36-point operational questionnaire to receive a customized logistics proposal.
distributionPage.formSubtitle: Connect with vetted 3PL and distribution partners in Baja California.

contractPage.heroSubtitle: Don't want to run a factory? Partner with established manufacturers in Baja California who already have the equipment, certifications, and trained workforce.
contractPage.establishedDesc: Skip the 2-year ramp-up. Partner with manufacturers already running production.
contractPage.ipDesc: NDA-first approach. Contract manufacturers handle production, you own the designs.
contractPage.certifiedDesc: We match you with CMs that already hold your required industry certifications.
contractPage.workforceDesc: Baja California has 700,000+ manufacturing workers with decades of export experience.
contractPage.medicalDesc: Clean room assembly (ISO Class 7/8) for Class I, II, and III devices. ISO 13485 certified.
contractPage.electronicsDesc: PCB assembly, SMT lines, wire harness, and consumer electronics manufacturing.
contractPage.aerospaceDesc: Precision components, sub-assemblies, and MRO services for commercial and defense.
contractPage.automotiveDesc: Sub-assembly, upholstery, injection molding, and wire harness for OEMs and Tier 1/2 suppliers.
contractPage.furnitureDesc: Quality-focused contract manufacturing for commercial/retail builds with finish consistency and packaging readiness.
contractPage.cncDesc: AS9100 registered precision machining for tight-tolerance aerospace and commercial applications.
contractPage.regionsSubtitle: Primary regions: Baja California and Monterrey / Nuevo León, with broader contract manufacturing in Mexico coverage based on process and certifications.
contractPage.step1Desc: Share your product specs, volume needs, quality standards, and certification requirements.
contractPage.step2Desc: We curate 3-5 contract manufacturers that match your criteria, all pre-vetted.
contractPage.step3Desc: Tour facilities, meet management, assess capabilities, and review quality systems.
contractPage.step4Desc: We assist with contract terms, pricing negotiation, and production ramp-up.
contractPage.faq1Question: What minimum order volumes do CMs require?
contractPage.faq1Answer: This varies by industry and complexity. Medical device CMs often start at $500K-$1M annual spend. Electronics CMs may accept lower volumes for high-mix products. We match you with partners suited to your scale.
contractPage.faq2Answer: All relationships begin with NDAs. Mexican IP law is USMCA-aligned, providing strong protections. We recommend registering patents/trademarks in Mexico as well. CMs typically sign non-compete clauses for your product category.
contractPage.faq3Question: Can I audit the factory before committing?
contractPage.faq3Answer: Absolutely. We encourage and facilitate site visits. Most clients conduct 1-2 day audits covering quality systems, production capabilities, workforce, and financial stability before making a decision.
contractPage.faq4Question: What certifications should I look for?
contractPage.faq4Answer: Medical: ISO 13485, FDA Registration. Automotive: IATF 16949. Aerospace: AS9100D, NADCAP. Electronics: IPC-A-610, ISO 9001. We only match you with CMs holding your required certifications.
contractPage.formSubtitle: Tell us your product, volume, and certification requirements. We'll curate 3-5 pre-vetted contract manufacturers.
contractPage.strategySubtitle: We move beyond simple introductions. We meticulously vet suppliers and protect your IP to de-risk your entry into Mexico.
contractPage.capabilityDesc: Deep-dive surveys into engineering, quality systems, and financial health—not just a name and a website.
contractPage.roadmapDesc: Plan your 'de-risking' strategy: Start with a contract partner, then transition to your own facility as you scale.
contractPage.safeguardsDesc: We act as your local eyes and ears, managing pricing, pilot runs, and routine audits to ensure US standards.

realEstatePage.heroSubtitle: Secure the perfect industrial space for your manufacturing or logistics operation in Mexico's top border city.
realEstatePage.premiumText1: Baja California offers over 70 million square feet of industrial inventory. Whether you need a 20,000 sq. ft. light assembly facility or a 200,000 sq. ft. distribution center, we help you navigate the market to find the right property.
realEstatePage.premiumText2: We have relationships with all major industrial developers in the region, including Vesta, Prologis, and local groups, ensuring you get access to both on-market and off-market opportunities.
realEstatePage.faq1Question: What are the current lease rates?
realEstatePage.faq1Answer: Lease rates in Baja California typically range from $0.75 to $0.95 USD per sq. ft. per month (NNN), depending on location and building class.
realEstatePage.faq2Question: What is the typical lease term?
realEstatePage.faq2Answer: Standard industrial lease terms are usually 3 to 5 years, though longer terms (7-10 years) may be required for built-to-suit projects.
realEstatePage.formSubtitle: Tell us your requirements and we'll send you a curated list of available properties.
realEstatePage.strategySubtitle: More than just finding a building. We perform technical audits and comparative studies to ensure your site supports your long-term operation.
realEstatePage.utilityDesc: We verify substation capacity and water availability before you sign, mitigating the risk of utility shortages.
realEstatePage.comparativeDesc: TCO analysis comparing Baja California vs. Bajío or other regions to quantify your total landed cost.
realEstatePage.tenantDesc: Expert advice on Build-to-Suit vs. Existing Inventory to maximize leverage in a low-vacancy market.

callCenterPage.heroSubtitle: Scale your customer support operations with Baja California's world-class bilingual workforce at a fraction of US costs.
callCenterPage.costSavingsDesc: Reduce operational costs compared to US-based call centers while maintaining quality.
callCenterPage.agentsDesc: Access a deep talent pool of English and Spanish speaking professionals.
callCenterPage.timezoneDesc: Same working hours as California for real-time collaboration and oversight.
callCenterPage.cultureDesc: Agents familiar with US culture and customer expectations.
callCenterPage.glanceTitle: Baja California BPO at a Glance
callCenterPage.formSubtitle: Tell us about your support needs and we'll connect you with vetted call center partners.

s321.heroSubtitle: Navigate the 2025 de minimis suspension and discover compliant Section 321 distribution strategies in Mexico.
s321.whatIsDesc: Section 321 has historically been a massive advantage for e-commerce. However, recent US Customs suspensions have changed the rules. Understanding the pivot to IMMEX and Bonded strategies is now mandatory for cost-effective fulfillment.
s321.suspensionDesc: CBP has fully suspended Section 321 de minimis for commercial shipments. All B2C entries now require full customs processing, making 'Alternative Routing' the new standard.
s321.pivotDesc: Utilize Mexico's IMMEX program to defer duties and manage consolidated container entries effectively.
s321.complianceDesc: Ensure your supply chain remains compliant with the new Type 86 restrictions and USMCA 2026 protocols.
s321.whyBaja1: 20 Minutes from San Diego border crossings
s321.whyBaja2: Access to low-cost, high-quality Mexican logistics labor
s321.whyBaja3: Same-day injection into the US domestic shipping system
s321.whyBaja4: Significant savings on returns processing and reverse logistics
s321.step1Desc: Your goods arrive at the Port of Ensenada or Port of Long Beach in 'Bonded' status.
s321.step2Desc: Kitting, labeling, and storage in a world-class Tijuana facility without paying duties.
s321.step3Desc: B2C orders are prepared as consolidated entries under new regulatory protocols.
s321.step4Desc: Processed via specialized lanes and handed over to US carriers in San Diego.
s321.formSubtitle: Schedule a call to discuss your 2026 de minimis strategy and alternative routing.

broker.networkDesc: Direct access to 70+ industrial parks and top-tier logistics operators.

questionnaire.title: 3PL Operation Questionnaire
questionnaire.subtitle: Confidential Assessment
questionnaire.intro: Please provide the following information to help us understand your operation requirements.
questionnaire.q1: How many square feet do you need for your operation?
questionnaire.q2: What kind of products would you be moving with us?
questionnaire.q3: What is the presentation of the product we will be receiving? (standard pallets, bulk, bags, packages, etc.)
questionnaire.q4: Please provide the dimensions in inches of the packages or pallets we will be receiving (length, width, height).
questionnaire.q5: What is the average weight in pounds of the pallets?
questionnaire.q6: What are the tariff fractions of the products we would be storing?
questionnaire.q7: Are any of the products Hazmat?
questionnaire.q8: How many levels can the pallets be stacked one on top of the other?
questionnaire.q9: How many orders are we going to process per day?
questionnaire.q10: How many pieces does a typical order consist of?
questionnaire.q11: Are we going to do virtual transfers or are we going to do home extension?
questionnaire.q12: Explain the process you need to be carried out within the Distribution Center. (Attach diagram/doc if needed)
questionnaire.q13: How will you transmit the orders to us?
questionnaire.q14: What is the order management system you use?
questionnaire.q15: How often would you be transmitting the orders to us?
questionnaire.q16: What would be the order transmission protocol? (AS2, FTP, SFTP, Mail, OTHER)
questionnaire.q17: What will be the content of the transmission? (EDI ANSI X12, PLAIN TEXT CSV/TAB/PIPE)
questionnaire.q18: Please provide an example of the commands you would transmit to us for testing.
questionnaire.q19: Do you have an estimate of the number of personnel required?
questionnaire.q20: Do you have an estimate of the number of pallets positions to store?
questionnaire.q21: How many trailer loads will we receive per week?
questionnaire.q22: How many pallets will fit in each trailer load?
questionnaire.q23: How many orders or units will we be shipping per day?
questionnaire.q24: Are there any current operations we can visit?
questionnaire.q25: How many part numbers go on an average order?
questionnaire.q26: How many SKU's do you handle in total? (Attach catalog if possible)
questionnaire.q27: How many units/cartons fit on a standard 40"x48"x60" pallet?
questionnaire.q28: Do you need special equipment? (sewing machines, cutting machines, etc.)
questionnaire.q29: What are the destinations we would be shipping to?
questionnaire.q30: What is the company that owns the merchandise?
questionnaire.q31: Which company would we bill for our services?
questionnaire.q32: What would be the total value of the merchandise to be held?
questionnaire.q33: Do you agree to extend your policy to insure the merchandise?
questionnaire.q34: What is the period for which you require our services?
questionnaire.q35: Can we offer you quotes for transportation? What are the addresses?
questionnaire.q36: What Annex 24 system do you use?

aboutPage.mainHeading: Turning "we want Mexico" into a clear next step
aboutPage.mainSub: Nearshore Navigator exists to make decisions easier: grounded guidance, steady follow-through, and execution support that matches your reality.
aboutPage.struggleDesc: Nearshoring decisions often get stuck after the initial excitement—when the real questions show up:
aboutPage.struggleList1: Where in Baja should we be?
aboutPage.struggleList2: What will the cost structure look like?
aboutPage.approachDesc: We’re not a directory and we’re not "one-size-fits-all." Every project has different constraints.
aboutPage.tier2Desc: Are you a mid-sized supplier feeling the squeeze? Big enough to need Mexico, but too lean to carry a 20-person launch team?
aboutPage.trapDesc: Trying to "copy-paste" a GM/Ford style expansion without their budget creates administrative chaos.
aboutPage.fixDesc: We structure "Shelter" agreements that let you own the equipment and process, while someone else handles the HR/Import headaches.
aboutPage.denisseBio1: With over a decade of experience driving economic development and manufacturing growth in the Cali-Baja region, Denisse brings a strategic, data-driven approach to nearshoring.
aboutPage.denisseBio2: Having supported the launch and growth of manufacturing operations across the Cali-Baja corridor, she understands that successful expansion isn't just about finding a building — it's about building a sustainable operation. Her experience spans economic development marketing, site selection analysis, and navigating the cross-border cultural landscape.
aboutPage.denisseBio3: At Nearshore Navigator, Denisse leads with a "client-first" philosophy, ensuring that every recommendation is backed by ground-truth data and aligned with your long-term business goals.
aboutPage.toolkitDesc: Companies don’t all enter Mexico the same way. We help you evaluate the best route—and support the steps that turn the decision into a launch.
aboutPage.cardRealEstateDesc: Shortlisting facility options that fit your requirements, coordinating productive site tours, building decision-ready comparisons, and supporting negotiation steps.
aboutPage.cardContractDesc: We help you start correctly: define requirements, connect with qualified vendors, and drive evaluation and follow-up so the process doesn’t stall.
aboutPage.cardShelterServicesDesc: When speed matters. We help you assess the most efficient entry route (Shelter, M&A, or Standalone) and present vetted options.
aboutPage.processTitle: A no-BS process that keeps projects moving
aboutPage.step2Desc: The most practical route for your situation
aboutPage.step5Desc: Introductions and support to move into execution
aboutPage.controlText: You stay in control. We bring structure, realism, and momentum.
aboutPage.bajaDesc: We support projects across Baja California. The right location depends on what you need—labor profile, border strategy, infrastructure, supplier adjacency, operating model, and timeline. Our job is to help you align those variables and make a smart decision faster.
aboutPage.finalTitle: Let’s map your best path into Baja California
aboutPage.finalDesc: Tell us what you’re building and when you need to be operational. We’ll help you identify the smartest starting point—and the next steps to move forward.

caseStudies.title: Expansion Projects We've Supported
caseStudies.subtitle: Real outcomes for manufacturers who moved forward with structure and the right partnerships.
caseStudies.case1_title: Tier-1 Harness Supplier Enters Mexico in 90 Days
caseStudies.case1_challenge: A California-based Tier-1 aerospace wire harness manufacturer needed to reduce production costs without disrupting delivery timelines or their AS9100 certification requirements.
caseStudies.case1_solution: We structured a shelter arrangement in Mexicali, matched them with a facility already running aerospace processes, and managed the entire transition timeline from evaluation to live production.
caseStudies.case1_result1: 42% reduction in direct labor cost vs. California baseline
caseStudies.case1_result2: Operations live within 90 days of initial assessment
caseStudies.case1_result3: Zero certification gaps — AS9100 maintained throughout
caseStudies.case2_title: Startup Finds ISO 13485 Contract Partner Without a Site Visit Headache
caseStudies.case2_challenge: An early-stage medical device company needed a contract manufacturer for Class II device assembly but lacked the internal resources to vet and audit facilities in Mexico.
caseStudies.case2_solution: We defined their spec requirements, pre-screened 12 potential partners down to 4, organized structured factory audits, and supported NDA and contract negotiations.
caseStudies.case2_result1: Selected ISO 13485-certified CM with FDA registration already in place
caseStudies.case2_result2: Pilot production launched within 6 months of first call
caseStudies.case2_result3: Full IP protection framework established before production started
caseStudies.case3_title: DTC Furniture Brand Saves $1.2M vs. Asian Reshoring
caseStudies.case3_challenge: A direct-to-consumer furniture brand was evaluating reshoring from Vietnam due to tariff exposure and long lead times. They initially assumed domestic US manufacturing was the only alternative.
caseStudies.case3_solution: We presented a Baja California contract manufacturing option with total landed cost analysis, side-by-side against US domestic and remaining in Vietnam. Coordinated two facility tours and led the commercial negotiation.
caseStudies.case3_result1: $1.2M projected 3-year savings vs. US domestic production
caseStudies.case3_result2: Lead times cut from 14 weeks (Asia) to 5 days (Tijuana to LA)
caseStudies.case3_result3: No tariff exposure — USMCA qualified from day one

bio_denisse.title: Meet Denisse Martinez | Nearshore Consultant in Baja California
bio_denisse.intro: A Strategic Approach to Mexico Manufacturing
bio_denisse.backgroundDesc: With over 15 years of experience across the Cali-Baja corridor, Denisse Martinez has established herself as a leading voice in North American nearshoring. By combining ground-truth economic data with deep regional ties, she guides Fortune 500s and mid-market manufacturers through the complexities of cross-border expansion. Her strategic vision goes beyond mere real estate transactions, focusing on holistic supply chain integration and sustainable operations.
bio_denisse.trackRecordTitle: Supported Over 20 Companies Across Multiple Industries
bio_denisse.expertiseDesc: Denisse advocates for an objective brokerage model, ensuring that US manufacturers receive unbiased guidance rather than being shoehorned into one-size-fits-all shelter models. Her methodology prioritizes strict compliance, risk mitigation, and long-term operational resilience in Baja California.
bio_denisse.quote: Nearshoring decisions often get stuck after the initial excitement—when the real questions show up. My job is to turn 'we want Mexico' into a clear next step.
bio_denisse.imageAlt: Denisse Martinez, Nearshore Consultant in Baja California
bio_denisse.certNetworkDesc: We exclusively work with certified facilities and proven partners across Baja California.

hero.title: The Ultimate Guide to Manufacturing in Tijuana
hero.subtitle: Positioned 20 minutes from San Diego, Tijuana offers 0% USMCA tariffs, $7.84/hr fully-burdened labor, and 1-day shipping to California. See why top US manufacturers are bypassing Asia.
hero.trust_label: Trusted by leaders operating in Baja California

cost_advantage.title: The "China-Plus-One" Pivot: The Cost Advantage
cost_advantage.subtitle: The math driving the massive shift in global supply chains from Shenzhen to Tijuana.
cost_advantage.labor_desc: Inclusive of IMSS social security, INFONAVIT housing, vacation premiums, and mandatory profit-sharing, representing a 60-75% reduction vs California.
cost_advantage.real_estate_desc: With an 8% vacancy rate in Q1 2026, premium industrial space is abundant. Mesa de Otay premium zones offer 32ft clear heights and heavy power.
cost_advantage.tariffs_desc: Bypass the 25-100% Section 301 tariffs on Chinese imports entirely. Goods manufactured under IMMEX entering the US are inherently duty-free.

clusters.title: Dominant Industry Clusters
clusters.subtitle: The infrastructure, supply chains, and specialized talent pools are already here.

medical.title: Medical Devices
medical.desc: Tijuana is the largest medical device hub in Mexico and second largest globally. Over 70 FDA-registered facilities operate in Class-A parks, producing everything from Class I disposables to Class III pacemakers.

aerospace.title: Aerospace & Defense
aerospace.desc: Bolstered by its proximity to Southern California`s defense sector, Tijuana has developed deep capabilities in precision machining, composite structures, and ITAR compliance.

electronics.title: Electronics & EV
electronics.desc: The original DNA of Tijuana`s maquiladora era. The city remains a global powerhouse for PCBA (SMT lines), automotive wire harnesses, and EV component assembly.

real_estate.title: Class-A Industrial Parks
real_estate.subtitle: Tijuana operates like a fortress for manufacturers. Industrial zones are physically separated from residential areas, maintaining 24/7 security.
real_estate.pacific_desc: The premier location. Immediate Otay Mesa access.
real_estate.otay_desc: Heavy power availability. Highest demand sector.

border.title: The Otay Mesa Border Advantage
border.desc: The FAST Lane program allows registered commercial carriers to bypass standard traffic, clearing customs in under 90 minutes.

models.title: How to Enter the Market
models.subtitle: The 90-day playbook vs. Turnkey Outsourcing.
models.shelter_desc: The fastest way to establish your own factory. A Mexican shelter company holds the IMMEX permit and handles compliance while you control production.
models.contract_desc: Turnkey outsourcing for immediate scale. Partner with an existing, ISO-certified manufacturer in Tijuana who already has the facility and team.

medical-devices.name: Medical Device Manufacturing
medical-devices.description: Scale FDA-compliant production in Tijuana and Mexicali's world-class med-tech clusters.
medical-devices.marketOpportunity: Baja California is the single largest medical device cluster in North America, with 70+ FDA-registered plants and 12,000+ operators.
medical-devices.laborProfile: Specialized in sterile packaging, Class II/III assembly, and ISO 13485 quality systems management.
medical-devices.logisticsEdge: 90-minute cold-chain transit from Tijuana production floors to San Diego distribution hubs.
medical-devices.clusterSub: Join the leading companies already operating in this region.
medical-devices.realEstateSub: These industrial parks are specifically engineered for {name} requirements.

aerospace.name: Aerospace & Defense
aerospace.description: Access the most advanced AS9100-certified manufacturing hub in Mexicali and Sonora.
aerospace.marketOpportunity: Mexicali is Mexico's aerospace capital, hosting the largest concentration of precision machining and avionics production.
aerospace.laborProfile: Highly technical workforce skilled in composite materials, CNC machining, and NADCAP processes.
aerospace.logisticsEdge: Direct integration with the Arizona and California aerospace 'Megaregions' (Raytheon, Boeing supply chains).
aerospace.clusterSub: Join the leading companies already operating in this region.
aerospace.realEstateSub: These industrial parks are specifically engineered for {name} requirements.

automotive.name: Automotive & Electric Vehicles
automotive.description: The backbone of the North American EV supply chain, from wire harnesses to battery assembly.
automotive.marketOpportunity: Juárez and the North-Central corridor dominate the global automotive wire harness and lighting assembly markets.
automotive.laborProfile: Stable, high-volume assembly expertise with JIT (Just-in-Time) delivery culture deeply embedded.
automotive.logisticsEdge: I-35 corridor connectivity allows 24-48 hour delivery to US auto hubs in Detroit and Tennessee.
automotive.clusterSub: Join the leading companies already operating in this region.
automotive.realEstateSub: These industrial parks are specifically engineered for {name} requirements.

electronics.name: Electronics & SMT
electronics.description: High-volume consumer and industrial electronics centering on Reynosa and Tijuana.
electronics.marketOpportunity: Reynosa and Tijuana are global leaders in TV, appliance, and telecommunications hardware assembly.
electronics.laborProfile: Precision SMT (Surface Mount Technology) operators and PCBA testing specialists.
electronics.logisticsEdge: Direct cross-border access to Texas and California logistics distributors.
electronics.clusterSub: Join the leading companies already operating in this region.
electronics.realEstateSub: These industrial parks are specifically engineered for {name} requirements.

distribution-3pl.name: Distribution & 3PL Logistics
distribution-3pl.description: Leverage the Laredo-Nuevo Laredo gateway, the busiest commercial land port in the Americas.
distribution-3pl.marketOpportunity: Nuevo Laredo handles 40% of all US-Mexico trade, making it the premier location for cross-docking and warehouse hubbing.
distribution-3pl.laborProfile: Bilingual logistics coordinators, warehouse management system (WMS) specialists, and certified customs agents.
distribution-3pl.logisticsEdge: Zero-wait intermodal rail access (Union Pacific/KCSM) to the US Midwest and East Coast.
distribution-3pl.clusterSub: Join the leading companies already operating in this region.
distribution-3pl.realEstateSub: These industrial parks are specifically engineered for {name} requirements.

tijuana.medical-devices: The world's second-largest medical device cluster. Tijuana specializes in Class II and III implantable devices and high-precision sterile assembly. Its proximity to San Diego's biotech hub allows for daily engineering exchange.
tijuana.aerospace: Tijuana's aerospace sector is deeply integrated with the Southern California defense corridor. Specializes in interior components, secondary structures, and precision machining.
tijuana.electronics: Tijuana's electronics sector focuses on high-mix, low-volume (HMLV) production and advanced PCBA for medical and aerospace applications.

mexicali.medical-devices: Mexicali offers a lower-cost alternative to Tijuana for med-tech, with a focus on high-volume disposables and diagnostic equipment assembly. It benefits from shorter border wait times at Calexico East.
mexicali.aerospace: Mexico's aerospace capital. Mexicali handles 70% of Gulfstream's jet assembly and is a global center for avionics testing and thermal management systems (Honeywell).

juarez.automotive: The global epicenter of wire harness manufacturing. Juárez is rapidly transitioning to EV architecture, producing complex high-voltage harnesses and electronic control units for the Big Three.
juarez.electronics: Foxconn and other major EMS players operate high-volume assembly lines in Juárez, feeding the North American consumer tech market.

monterrey.automotive: The future EV capital of Latin America. Monterrey captures 72% of Mexico's nearshoring investment, driven by the Tesla Gigafactory ecosystem and a massive Tier 1 supplier base.

reynosa.electronics: The 'Television Capital' of the world. Reynosa specializes in high-volume consumer electronics (LG, Panasonic) and industrial automation controls (Emerson).

nuevo-laredo.distribution-3pl: The 'World Trade' gateway. Nuevo Laredo handles 40% of all US-Mexico rail and truck freight. Ideal for large-scale cross-docking and multi-modal distribution into the US Midwest.

marketingPage.heroSubtitle: I work alongside your team as your part-time marketing director — bringing the strategy, tools, and execution that manufacturers need to grow. Pick exactly the services you need, nothing more.
marketingPage.servicesSubtitle: Choose exactly what your business needs right now. Start with one service, add more as you grow.
marketingPage.linkedinDesc: Targeted outreach to decision-makers in your industry. We identify, connect, and nurture prospects through personalized LinkedIn campaigns that generate real conversations.
marketingPage.crmDesc: Stop losing leads in spreadsheets. I audit your current CRM process, set up automated lead routing, scoring, and follow-up sequences so nothing falls through the cracks.
marketingPage.contentDesc: Monthly blog posts, case studies, and LinkedIn articles tailored to your industry. Position your company as a thought leader that engineers and buyers trust.
marketingPage.emailDesc: Custom-designed email sequences that nurture prospects from first touch to booked meeting. AI-powered personalization at scale with transparent deliverability tracking.
marketingPage.seoDesc: Comprehensive technical SEO audit, keyword strategy, and on-page optimization. Get found by buyers actively searching for your services on Google.
marketingPage.socialDesc: Consistent, professional social media presence across LinkedIn, Instagram, and Facebook. Showcase your capabilities with engaging posts and short-form video content.
marketingPage.howSubtitle: No long-term contracts. No bloated retainers. Just the marketing help you actually need.
marketingPage.step1Desc: Choose the specific marketing services your business needs right now. No bundles, no commitments — just what moves the needle.
marketingPage.step2Desc: I work alongside your people as your part-time marketing director — weekly strategy calls, hands-on execution, and full transparency.
marketingPage.step3Desc: Live reporting on every campaign. You see exactly what's working, what's not, and where every dollar goes. No black boxes.
marketingPage.whyDenisseTitle: Why Work With a Fractional Marketing Director?
marketingPage.whyPoint1: Senior marketing leadership without the $200K+ salary
marketingPage.whyPoint2: Someone who understands manufacturing, not just marketing buzzwords
marketingPage.whyPoint4: Scale up or down anytime — no long-term contracts required
marketingPage.formSubtitle: Book a free 15-minute call. No pitch, no pressure — just an honest conversation about what's working and what's not.
